canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, "Long-context understanding" is predominantly framed as a latent ability of models to effectively process, comprehend, and utilize information from extended input sequences, often far exceeding their original training context windows. This construct is referred to by various names, including "long-context comprehension," "long-range reasoning," and "long-context capability," all pointing to the challenge of maintaining performance and coherence over lengthy inputs. The scope of "long context" is broad, encompassing single documents exceeding a page, conversational histories, and inputs spanning from 16K to 128K tokens. While most definitions focus on textual data, a notable subset extends the concept to multi-modal scenarios, defining specific facets like "long-form video understanding," "long image-sequence understanding," and the comprehension of documents containing text, tables, and images. A recurring theme across definitions is the model's capacity to not only process but also integrate relevant information, maintain coherence, and capture what some papers term "long-range dependencies" between distant elements in a sequence. The field operationalizes and measures this construct primarily through performance on specialized benchmarks. The most prevalent measurement instruments cited are LongBench, described as a "comprehensive, bilingual, multitask benchmark," and RULER, a synthetic benchmark for evaluating long-context models. Other frequently used benchmarks for textual understanding include InfiniteBench, L-Eval, and BABILong, while multi-modal abilities are assessed using instruments like EgoSchema for video and MMLongBench-Doc for visually-rich documents. This construct is studied alongside several related concepts, including length generalization, planning, and complex reasoning, and some work suggests that deficits in long-range reasoning can contribute to model hallucination.

definitions.text: The latent ability to comprehend and process lengthy or syntactically complex textual inputs, ranging from short lines to documents exceeding one page.
definitions.paper: SmartPlay : A Benchmark for LLMs as Intelligent Agents

definitions.text: The latent ability of a language model to maintain coherence, relevance, and performance when processing input sequences far exceeding its original context window.
definitions.paper: Hierarchical Context Merging: Better Long Context Understanding for Pre-trained LLMs

definitions.text: The latent ability of a model to effectively process and utilize information across extended input sequences, integrating relevant content despite length and noise.
definitions.paper: A Real-World WebAgent with Planning, Long Context Understanding, and Program Synthesis

definitions.text: The capacity of a model to process, retain, and utilize information from extended conversational histories or long input sequences.
definitions.paper: MMDU: A Multi-Turn Multi-Image Dialog Understanding Benchmark and Instruction-Tuning Dataset for LVLMs

definitions.text: The model's comprehensive ability to read, process, and reason about information presented in extremely long documents or sequences.
definitions.paper: InfLLM: Training-Free Long-Context Extrapolation for LLMs with an Efficient Context Memory

definitions.text: The ability to collect, synthesize, and reason over information and evidence distributed across multiple different pages of a document.
definitions.paper: MMLONGBENCH-DOC: Benchmarking Long-context Document Understanding with Visualizations

definitions.text: The latent ability to comprehend and answer questions about lengthy, multi-page documents containing mixed modalities such as text, tables, charts, and images.
definitions.paper: SV-RAG: LoRA-Contextualizing Adaptation of  MLLMs for Long Document Understanding

definitions.text: The latent ability of a model to effectively process, understand, and perform tasks requiring information from extended textual contexts.
definitions.paper: LongPO: Long Context Self-Evolution of Large Language Models through Short-to-Long Preference Optimization

definitions.text: The latent ability to process, comprehend, and synthesize information to generate responses from extensive textual inputs.
definitions.paper: Law of the Weakest Link: Cross Capabilities of Large Language Models

definitions.text: The model's ability to effectively process, understand, and utilize information from very long sequences of text.

definitions.text: The ability of a model to effectively process, understand, and utilize information from very long input sequences to perform complex tasks.
definitions.paper: OmniKV: Dynamic Context Selection for Efficient Long-Context LLMs

definitions.text: A specific facet of video understanding focused on comprehending videos of extended duration, often requiring the integration of information over many minutes.
definitions.paper: Streaming Video Question-Answering with In-context Video KV-Cache Retrieval

definitions.text: A specific facet of multi-modal understanding focused on the model's ability to comprehend and reason about the content and temporal dynamics of extended video sequences.
definitions.paper: LongVILA: Scaling Long-Context Visual Language Models for Long Videos

definitions.text: The model's latent ability to process and comprehend information from extended sequences of images, such as those found in lengthy videos or multi-image documents.
definitions.paper: mPLUG-Owl3: Towards Long Image-Sequence Understanding in Multi-Modal Large Language Models

definitions.text: The ability of a model to effectively process and utilize information from a large number of visual inputs presented simultaneously in its context window.
definitions.paper: Visual Haystacks: A Vision-Centric Needle-In-A-Haystack Benchmark

definitions.text: The latent capability to comprehend first-person videos by recognizing activities, objects, and other visual details from a self-centered viewpoint.
definitions.paper: MMEgo: Towards Building Egocentric Multimodal LLMs for Video QA

definitions.text: The latent ability of a model to capture long-range dependencies and process extended input sequences effectively.
definitions.paper: Quest: Query-centric Data Synthesis Approach for Long-context Scaling of Large Language Model

definitions.text: The ability of an LLM to retain and effectively utilize information from extended dialogue histories, despite the constraints of finite context length.
definitions.paper: DenseLoRA: Dense Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

definitions.text: The latent ability of a language model to effectively process and retain information across very long input sequences, maintaining coherence and performance over thousands of tokens.
definitions.paper: Beyond Logits: Aligning Feature Dynamics for Effective Knowledge Distillation

definitions.text: The model's underlying capacity to retrieve specific, relevant information from long input contexts, especially in information-sparse scenarios.
definitions.paper: VMLUBenchmarks: A comprehensive benchmark toolkit forVietnameseLLMs

definitions.text: The model's capacity to capture and utilize relationships between distant tokens in a sequence, crucial for coherent processing of long texts.
definitions.paper: HyKGE: A Hypothesis Knowledge Graph EnhancedRAGFramework for Accurate and Reliable MedicalLLMs Responses

definitions.text: The latent ability of LLMs to maintain understanding and retain information across extended input sequences, particularly challenged by structural complexity and length.
definitions.paper: The Tug of War Within: Mitigating the Fairness-Privacy Conflicts in Large Language Models

definitions.text: The model's ability to retain and correctly use information across long or multi-step planning tasks, particularly in handling user profiles and extended task descriptions without losing critical details.
definitions.paper: Analyzing and Mitigating Inconsistency in Discrete Speech Tokens for Neural Codec Language Models

definitions.text: The latent ability of a VLM to maintain coherent understanding and performance across extended sequences of multimodal inputs, particularly over long videos or interleaved images.
definitions.paper: Just Go Parallel: Improving the Multilingual Capabilities of Large Language Models

definitions.text: The latent capacity of a model to maintain coherent and accurate representations over long input sequences, particularly in preserving positional information across distant tokens.
definitions.paper: The Hidden Attention of Mamba Models

definitions.text: The capacity to synthesize information across multiple documents or abstracts that are distantly related in the input sequence, maintaining coherence and relevance in the output.
definitions.paper: How does Misinformation Affect Large Language Model Behaviors and Preferences?

definitions.text: The comprehensive ability to process, comprehend, and reason about visual and linguistic information presented in videos spanning long durations, typically an hour or more.
definitions.paper: HourVideo: 1-Hour Video-Language Understanding

definitions.text: The task of processing and comprehending lengthy text documents to extract information or answer questions.
definitions.paper: LV-XAttn: Distributed Cross-Attention for Long Visual Inputs in Multimodal Large Language Models

definitions.text: The ability to effectively process, understand, and draw inferences from extended passages of text or data.
definitions.paper: MapEval: A Map-Based Evaluation of Geo-Spatial Reasoning in Foundation Models

definitions.text: The latent ability to access and utilize information from distant parts of the input sequence, beyond the immediate attention window.
definitions.paper: Efficient Length-Generalizable Attention via Causal Retrieval for Long-Context Language Modeling

definitions.text: The model's ability to maintain accuracy and coherence when processing and generating text with very long input sequences.
definitions.paper: Puzzle: Distillation-Based NAS for Inference-Optimized LLMs

definitions.text: The ability of a multi-modal model to improve its performance on a downstream task by conditioning on a long context of many demonstration examples.
definitions.paper: Efficient Multi-modal Long Context Learning for Training-free Adaptation

definitions.text: The latent ability of a model to process and maintain dependencies across extensive input contexts.
definitions.paper: LaCache: Ladder-Shaped KV Caching for Efficient Long-Context Modeling of Large Language Models

definitions.text: The capacity to process and use information across very long input sequences without losing task performance.
definitions.paper: XAttention: Block Sparse Attention with Antidiagonal Scoring

definitions.text: The model's ability to effectively process and utilize information from extensive input sequences.
definitions.paper: Efficient Long Context Fine-tuning with Chunk Flow

definitions.text: The ability to synthesize and reason over information distributed across extended input sequences, integrating distant pieces of information to form coherent responses.
definitions.paper: In-Context Pretraining: Language Modeling Beyond Document Boundaries

definitions.text: The latent ability to maintain coherent and effective reasoning over multiple interaction rounds to achieve a distant goal.
definitions.paper: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents

definitions.text: The model's capacity to model relationships and dependencies between elements that are far apart in a sequence.
definitions.paper: Orchid: Flexible and Data-Dependent Convolution for Sequence Modeling

definitions.text: The ability to model and reason over long sequences of information, capturing dependencies between distant elements.
definitions.paper: Theoretical Foundations of Deep Selective State-Space Models

definitions.text: The ability to capture dependencies and interactions across extended spatial and temporal horizons in traffic trajectories.
definitions.paper: BehaviorGPT: Smart Agent Simulation for Autonomous Driving with Next-Patch Prediction

definitions.text: The model's ability to identify and utilize relationships between pieces of information that are far apart within a long sequence of text.
definitions.paper: InfLLM: Training-Free Long-Context Extrapolation for LLMs with an Efficient Context Memory

definitions.text: The ability of a model to capture, represent, and utilize relationships between elements that are far apart in a sequence.
definitions.paper: MambaTree: Tree Topology is All You Need in State Space Model

definitions.text: The ability to handle extended conversation histories and long queries when generating useful responses.
definitions.paper: WildBench: Benchmarking LLMs with Challenging Tasks from Real Users in the Wild

definitions.text: The ability of a model to capture and utilize relationships between elements that are far apart in a sequence.
definitions.paper: Sparse Learning for State Space Models on Mobile

definitions.text: The capacity to process and understand the procedural steps in a program where the order of operations significantly affects the final semantic meaning.
definitions.paper: Can Large Language Models Understand Symbolic Graphics Programs?

definitions.text: The ability of a model to generate and maintain a coherent, multi-step plan to achieve a complex goal over an extended sequence of actions.
definitions.paper: SAFE: Schema-Driven Approximate Distance Join for Efficient Knowledge Graph Querying

characterization: Across the surveyed literature, "Long-context understanding" is predominantly framed as a latent ability of models to effectively process, comprehend, and utilize information from extended input sequences, often far exceeding their original training context windows. This construct is referred to by various names, including "long-context comprehension," "long-range reasoning," and "long-context capability," all pointing to the challenge of maintaining performance and coherence over lengthy inputs. The scope of "long context" is broad, encompassing single documents exceeding a page, conversational histories, and inputs spanning from 16K to 128K tokens. While most definitions focus on textual data, a notable subset extends the concept to multi-modal scenarios, defining specific facets like "long-form video understanding," "long image-sequence understanding," and the comprehension of documents containing text, tables, and images. A recurring theme across definitions is the model's capacity to not only process but also integrate relevant information, maintain coherence, and capture what some papers term "long-range dependencies" between distant elements in a sequence. The field operationalizes and measures this construct primarily through performance on specialized benchmarks. The most prevalent measurement instruments cited are LongBench, described as a "comprehensive, bilingual, multitask benchmark," and RULER, a synthetic benchmark for evaluating long-context models. Other frequently used benchmarks for textual understanding include InfiniteBench, L-Eval, and BABILong, while multi-modal abilities are assessed using instruments like EgoSchema for video and MMLongBench-Doc for visually-rich documents. This construct is studied alongside several related concepts, including length generalization, planning, and complex reasoning, and some work suggests that deficits in long-range reasoning can contribute to model hallucination.
